Description: Deliver a single-member Neo4j database ready for client use.
V1 Justification: MVP entry point — single `Neo4j` CR Standalone ([BDR-001]).

Description: Deliver a clustered Neo4j topology with configured members and cluster readiness.
V1 Justification: MVP cluster — per-pool STS + scale ([BDR-002] [BDR-009]); minimal networking/storage.

Description: Install the operator and required Kubernetes resources so it can reconcile Neo4j custom resources.
V1 Justification: MVP — YAML install + single-namespace scope only ([BDR-003]).

Description: Continuously reconcile declared custom resources and maintain the expected state.
Expose status, conditions, errors, and readiness on managed resources.
Support safe upgrade of the operator without disrupting managed Neo4j workloads.
Remove operator components while preserving managed data and workloads by default.
Create and manage the RBAC required for the selected operator scope.
Provide logs and metrics for operator health, reconciliation errors, and operational visibility.
Deploy Neo4j Enterprise edition.
User confirms enterprise license agreement.
User deploys Enterprise in eval mode.
Deploy one Neo4j server.
Configure initial minimum number of members required for cluster formation.
Deploy a clustered Neo4j topology.
V1 Justification: MVP Cluster mode — primaries + optional analytics/read pools ([BDR-002]).

Description: Apply Neo4j configuration, JVM settings, environment variables, and optional APOC configuration.
Manage initial password, existing password secret, secret mounts, image pull secrets, and sensitive configuration.
V1 Justification: MVP — password Secret + `secretMounts` / `additionalMounts`; operator-created registry Secret deferred.

Description: Configure TLS for Bolt, HTTPS, and cluster communication, including cert mounts and reload behavior.
V1 Justification: MVP — BYO via `spec.trust`: cluster (Cluster mode); bolt/https (Cluster + Standalone). cert-manager / reload deferred.

Description: Provision data and auxiliary volumes (Dynamic / Existing / Share), plus additionalMounts and secretMounts ([BDR-005]).
V1 Justification: MVP — Dynamic data + Existing (claimName/volume/VCT+selector) + aux Share/Dynamic/Existing. Cloud object storage deferred.

Description: Configure client Service for Bolt and HTTP; operator-derived internals in Cluster mode.
V1 Justification: MVP — ClusterIP default; NodePort/LoadBalancer + HTTPS/bolt expose supported via `connectivity.service`.

Description: Configure node selectors, affinity, anti-affinity, tolerations, topology spread, priority class, service account, and graceful shutdown.
Configure startup, readiness, and liveness probes appropriate for Neo4j startup, recovery, and cluster formation.
Detect configuration changes and apply them through controlled restarts or rolling updates.
V1 Justification: MVP — controlled restart on config change.

Description: Add or remove cluster members safely and enable servers when required.
Upgrade Neo4j image/version safely with validation and rollback considerations.
V1 Justification: Deferred — Neo4j rolling upgrade post-MVP; `spec.version` set at install only.

Description: Support on-demand and scheduled backups with configurable backup storage and service exposure.
V1 Justification: Out of V1 — backup feature + `features.backup` + neo4j-admin chart deferred.

Description: Restore Neo4j from a backup or seed URI, including optional secret mounts for cloud storage.
V1 Justification: Out of V1 — tied to backup/restore workflows.

Description: Enable metrics and ServiceMonitor integration when Prometheus Operator is available.
Configure server logs, user logs, query logs, HTTP logs, security logs, and log storage.
V1 Justification: MVP — image defaults (LOG-01); custom Log4j via `spec.logging.serverLogsXml` / `userLogsXml` (LOG-02).

Description: Support offline maintenance mode and maintenance jobs such as dump/load, consistency checks, and backup verification.
Delete Neo4j Kubernetes resources while preserving PVCs by default and optionally cleaning up services/jobs/PVCs.
V1 Justification: MVP — CR delete preserves data PVCs by default.

Install, upgrade, and uninstall operator using Helm chart.
